title: drimia ciliata
intro: Drimia ciliata, also known as the wild garlic of the veld or horse's hoof, is a plant species that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Drimia ciliata include relieving symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Drimia ciliata is often prepared as a herbal infusion or decoction, and can also be used as a poultice or topical application for skin conditions.
